segments.text: Then Jesus began to speak to them using illustrated stories. {
Once there was a man who planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a pit for a winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to some farmers, and left on a journey.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When harvest-time came, he sent one of his servants to the tenant farmers to collect some of the grapes from the vineyard.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they grabbed hold of him, beat him up, and sent him away with nothing.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So the man sent another servant. They hit him over the head and abused him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He sent another servant, and this one they killed. He sent many other servants, and they beat some of them and killed others.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the end the only one left was his son whom he loved, and eventually he sent him, thinking ‘They will respect my son.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But the farmers said to themselves, ‘Here's the owner's heir—if we kill him, we can get what he would have inherited!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they took him and killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now what is the owner of the vineyard going to do? He will come and kill those farmers, and then he will lease the vineyard to others.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Haven't you even read this Scripture: ‘The stone rejected by the builders has become the chief cornerstone.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is from the Lord, and it's marvelous to see!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Jewish leaders tried to have him arrested because they realized that the illustration was directed at them, but they were afraid of the crowd. So they left him alone and went away.
Later they sent some Pharisees with some of Herod's supporters to Jesus in an attempt to catch him out by what he said.
They arrived and said, {

Teacher, we know you are a truthful person and you don't look for approval, because you don't care about status or position.  Instead you teach God's way in accordance with the truth. So is it right to pay tribute to Caesar or not?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Should we pay up, or should we refuse?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus, realizing how hypocritical they were, asked them,
Why are you trying to catch me out? Bring me a coin to look at.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They gave him a coin. {
Whose is this image, and whose inscription?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked them.
Caesar's,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they replied.
Then give back to Caesar what belongs to him, and give back to God what belongs to him,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told them. They were amazed at his reply.
Then the Sadducees, who deny the resurrection, came and asked a question:
Teacher, Moses instructed us that if a man dies, leaving his widow childless, then his brother should marry his wife, and have children by her on his behalf.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once there were seven brothers. The first one got married, and then died without having children.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The second married his widow, and then died, childless. The third did the same.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In fact all seven died without having children. In the end the woman died too.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be, because she was the wife of all seven brothers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told them, {
This proves you're mistaken, and that you don't know the Scriptures or the power of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the dead rise, they don't marry, and aren't given in marriage. They're like the angels in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But concerning the resurrection, haven't you read in Moses' writings the story of the burning bush, where God spoke to Moses and told him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He's not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are completely mistaken!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: One of the religious teachers came and heard them arguing. He recognized that Jesus had given them a good answer. So he asked him, {
Which is the most important commandment of all?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
The first commandment is, ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God is one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your spirit, with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The second is ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’ There's no more important commandment than these.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's right, Teacher,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man replied.
It's true as you said that God is one, and there is no other.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: We are to love him with all our heart, all our understanding, and all our strength, and we are to love our neighbor as ourselves. This is far more important than offerings and sacrifices.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Jesus saw that he gave a thoughtful answer, and said, {
You're not far from the kingdom of God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After this no one was brave enough to ask him any more questions.
While Jesus was teaching in the Temple, he asked, {
Why do the religious teachers state that Christ is the son of David?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: David himself declared, inspired by the Holy Spirit, that the Lord said to my Lord, ‘Sit at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Since David himself calls him Lord, how can he be David's son?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The large crowd listened to what Jesus said with great delight.
Jesus continued to teach them, saying, {
Beware of religious leaders! They love to walk around in long robes, to be greeted respectfully in the marketplaces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They love to have the most important seats in the synagogues, and the best places at banquets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They cheat widows out of what they own,  and cover up the kind of people they really are with long-winded prayers. They will receive severe condemnation in the judgment.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury collection box, watching people tossing in coins. Many of the rich were extravagantly throwing in a lot of money.
Then a poor widow came along and put in just two small coins.
He called his disciples together and told them, {
I tell you the truth: this poor widow has put in more than all the rest together.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All of them gave from their wealth what they had, but she gave from her poverty what she didn't have. She put in all she had to live on.
segments.speaker: Jesus
